html:
GraveDug Grave

Graves or Mounds are naturally-appearing objects in the world that can be found individually in Forests or in a large group in Graveyards (which are characterized by large shrouds of fog). They may be found with or without a nearby Headstone, though graves without headstones can only be found in graveyards. Graves can only be dug up once, and each world has a limited number of them.

Each grave dug with a Shovel has a 10% chance to spawn a Ghost, which will be aggressive towards the player. Otherwise, the player has a 50% chance of finding a random Trinket (4.17% chance for each individual Trinket), 15.35% chance each of a Blue or Red Gem and 3.07% chance each of Gears, a Life Giving Amulet, or Nightmare Fuel. Each grave dug costs the player 10 .

Headstone

Headstones are objects that usually spawn next to a Grave. In Don't Starve, they currently have no use (other than marking nearby Graves on the map) and cannot be destroyed.

Epitaphs

  • "Here lies some guy. Blah blah blah."
  • "This headstone is blank"
  • "It says: 'Milk. Eggs. Bacon.'"
  • "Hey that's my name!"
  • Developer names (Set Piece)
  • "Not sick, just coffin."
  • It says "Catch you later."
  • "Five more minutes."
  • "And don't come back."
  • "This world could not hold me."
  • It reads "Right behind you..." and "Made you look!"
  • "Quoth the tall bird, Evermore."
  • There's just a big "W" etched in it.
  • It says "Come on in."
  • It says "I guess this is my life now."
  • It says "Right where I belong."
  • An engravement of their last words: "Hey, watch this."
  • "It's not as bad as it looks."
  • It says "See you on the other side."
  • It says "These are just the pieces we could find."
  • It reads "You're standing on my face." Oops.
  • It says "Don't make fun. You'll be here too one day."
  • It reads "I can dig it." Oh.
  • It reads "Care to join me?"
  • It says "On to the next adventure."
  • It says "Finally, some peace and quiet."
  • An engravement of their last words: "That's enough of that."
  • "Whoops."
  • "I've better places to be."
  • An engravement of their last words: "This is fine."
  • The inscription reads "Always Watching". Odd.
  • It says "Mind your own business."
  • It's covered in a peaceful moss.
  • "Just needed a rest."
  • "Here lies some guy."
  • "Here lies some gal."
  • "Here lies some person."
  • "Age makes fools of us all."
  • It says "Help! get me out!" and "Nah, just kidding" beneath.
  • It says "I hope I turned the oven off."
  • "Sweet slumber."
  • "I didn't like it here anyway."
  • "I've better places to be."
  • "It's dirty down here."
  • "Just needed a rest."
  • "Don't even think about it."
  • It says "Help, get me out!" and "Nah, just kidding," beneath.
  • An engravement of their last words: "I love it so much I could just die!"
  • An engravement of their last words: "Uh-oh."
  • An engravement of their last words: "I'd rather die!"
  • An engravement of their last words: "Uh, okay bye."
  • It's cracked down the middle.
  • It says "The end is only the beginning".
  • It says "I was underground before it was a thing." Huh?
  • Someone left flowers.
  • It's old and worn.
  • It says "Did I make it into the history books?"
  • It says "I'm taking a nap. In the dirt."
  • It says "See you on the other side."
  • "It's worse than it looks."
  • "This is where I keep my stuff."
  • It says "I'm just pretending."
  • It reads "Is the coast clear yet?"
  • It says "Lost and Found".
  • "Beware." Beware of what?
  • It reads "I've made a grave error." Hm.
  • The headstone is blank.
  • It reads "Don't worry, it's cozy." That's nice.

Downloadable Content

In the Reign of Giants DLC, if a player has already dug up a Grave, they can bury an item called Webber's Skull inside the grave. Once this is done, light will shine on the mound, Webber will appear and jump into the grave, closing it, and finally, a large group of Spiders will appear. Additionally, Webber will be unlocked as a playable character if he has not been already. Doing this will also reset the grave, and it can be then dug up again for the normal loot.

Tips

  • Digging Graves is one of the quickest ways to go insane for farming Nightmare Fuel, although due to the limited number of Graves in the world, it is not a repeatable method.

Trivia

  • The gravestone epitaph "Always Watching" may be a reference to the video game Slender.
  • In a Shipwrecked connected world, graves will give trinkets from both the base game and Shipwrecked, more commonly dropping ones from the latter.
  • The gravestone epitaph "Pulvis Et Umbra Sumus" is Latin for "We are but dust and shadows" and is a phrase associated with the Cyclum Puzzles.
  • The gravestone epitaph "Quoth the Tallbird, Evermore" is a reference to the famous line from Edgar Allen Poe's poem, The Raven.
    • It also suggests they died from a Tallbird.

Gallery

  • Three Graves and a Headstone as seen in a Graveyard.
  • Headstones as seen on the Map.
  • Multiple Graves as seen in a Set Piece.
  • Graves found in the last level of Adventure Mode.
  • Webber unlocked from a Grave.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

GraveDug Grave
“I bet there's all sorts of good stuff down there!”
–Wilson, when examining a Grave.
“It's full of dead stuff, I bet.”
–Willow, when examining a Grave.
“Scary! Is probably full of bones!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Grave.
“Some day, I will join you.”
–Wendy, when examining a Grave.
“MEATLINGS ARE SO SUPERSTITIOUS”
–WX-78, when examining a Grave.
“A burial mound.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Grave.
“I should probably leave that alone.”
–Woodie, when examining a Grave.
“What lies beneath?”
–Maxwell, when examining a Grave.
“Could potentially yield secret property. And a poltergeist.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Grave.
“Are there treasures beneath the gravestönes?”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Grave.
“Our loot sense is tingling.”
–Webber, when examining a Grave.
“Seems rude to mess with it.”
–Walani, when examining a Grave.
“I cannot help wondering what might be down there.”
–Warly, when examining a Grave.
“Not a bad place ta hide yer silver.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Grave.
“WILBA CAN'ST DIG'D FOR TREASURES!”
–Wilba, when examining a Grave.
“Dirt hill”
–Wormwood, when examining a Grave.
“I'm just one bad adventure away from being a mound of dirt.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Grave.
“Anything good in there, ya think?”
–Winona, when examining a Grave.
“I sense no souls within.”
–Wortox, when examining a Grave.
“Hmm? Something buried here?”
–Wurt, when examining a Grave.
“I wonder if I'll find a skeleton down there...”
–Walter, when examining a Grave.
“You'll never catch me in one of those.”
–Wanda, when examining a Grave.
“I should probably feel bad about that.”
–Wilson, when examining a dug Grave.
“Better it than me.”
–Willow, when examining a dug Grave.
“I am sorry, dead peoples.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a dug Grave.
“The earth has vomited up its secrets.”
–Wendy, when examining a dug Grave.
“WORMS AND ICHOR”
–WX-78, when examining a dug Grave.
“A desecrated burial mound.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a dug Grave.
“Sorry! Don't haunt me!”
–Woodie, when examining a dug Grave.
“Already got that one.”
–Maxwell, when examining a dug Grave.
“Defiled for the sake of knowledge.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a dug Grave.
“I wanted the lööt!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a dug Grave.
“We're sorry about that.”
–Webber, when examining a dug Grave.
“Thanks for the loot, dude.”
–Walani, when examining a dug Grave.
“What have I become?”
–Warly, when examining a dug Grave.
“I had ta check fer treasure!”
–Woodlegs, when examining a dug Grave.
“HATH BEEN DUG'D”
–Wilba, when examining a dug Grave.
“Dug up”
–Wormwood, when examining a dug Grave.
“It's not like they were using it.”
–Wheeler, when examining a dug Grave.
“Just a hole now.”
–Winona, when examining a dug Grave.
“The dead had no use of it, you see.”
–Wortox, when examining a dug Grave.
“Someone left buncha bones in there, florp.”
–Wurt, when examining a dug Grave.
“Well, my curiosity is satisfied.”
–Walter, when examining a dug Grave.
“It's not like they were going to use any of that stuff.”
–Wanda, when examining a dug Grave.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Mộ hoặc Gò là những vật thể xuất hiện tự nhiên trên thế giới và có thể được tìm thấy riêng lẻ trong Rừng hoặc trong một nhóm lớn trong Nghĩa địa (được đặc trưng bởi những đám sương mù lớn). Chúng có thể được tìm thấy có hoặc không có Bia mộ gần đó, mặc dù những ngôi mộ không có bia mộ chỉ có thể được tìm thấy trong nghĩa địa. Mộ chỉ có thể được đào lên một lần và mỗi thế giới có số lượng mộ giới hạn.

'''Graves''' or '''Mounds''' are naturally-appearing objects in the world that can be found individually in [[Forest]]s or in a large group in [[Graveyard]]s (which are characterized by large shrouds of fog). They may be found with or without a nearby Headstone, though graves without headstones can only be found in graveyards. Graves can only be dug up once, and each world has a limited number of them.

Each grave dug with a [[Shovel]] has a 10% chance to spawn a [[Ghost]], which will be aggressive towards the player. Otherwise, the player has a 50% chance of finding a random [[Trinkets|Trinket]] (4.17% chance for each individual Trinket), 15.35% chance each of a [[Blue Gem|Blue]] or [[Red Gem]] and 3.07% chance each of [[Gears]], a [[Life Giving Amulet]], or [[Nightmare Fuel]]. Each grave dug costs the player 10 {{sanity}}.

=={{Pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content==
In the ''[[Reign of Giants icon|Reign of Giants]]'' DLC, if a player has already dug up a Grave, they can bury an item called [[Webber's Skull]] inside the grave. Once this is done, light will shine on the mound, [[Webber]] will appear and jump into the grave, closing it, and finally, a large group of [[Spiders]] will appear. Additionally, Webber will be unlocked as a playable character if he has not been already. Doing this will also reset the grave, and it can be then dug up again for the normal loot.
=={{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips ==
* Digging Graves is one of the quickest ways to go insane for farming [[Nightmare Fuel]], although due to the limited number of Graves in the world, it is not a repeatable method.

== {{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* The gravestone epitaph "Always Watching" may be a reference to the video game ''Slender''.
*In a ''Shipwrecked'' connected world, graves will give trinkets from both the base game and ''Shipwrecked'', more commonly dropping ones from the latter.
* The gravestone epitaph "Pulvis Et Umbra Sumus" is Latin for "We are but dust and shadows" and is a phrase associated with the [[Cyclum Puzzles]].
* The gravestone epitaph "Quoth the Tallbird, Evermore" is a reference to the famous line from Edgar Allen Poe's poem, ''[[wikipedia:The_Raven|The Raven]]''.
**It also suggests they died from a [[Tallbird]].
